HR

The Home RunChase

Catch Barry Bonds · 762

Build a 21-player lineup, one spin at a time. You won't see home run totals — only each batter's full stat line for the era and how many seasons they logged. Read the power, draft the boppers, and climb the all-time leaderboard to dethrone the King.

Hard Franchise

Spin a real team & era — the 1955 Dodgers, the '98 Yankees. Draft from that club's actual roster, reading power off their stat line. Brutal: even flawless picks reach 762 only ~7% of the time.

5 re-rolls · your lifeline
Play Franchise
Classic Open Draft

Spin an era & position and draft from the whole league at that spot. The record is in reach — if you can spot the slugger from a row of batting averages and RBIs. A pure test of ball knowledge.

5 re-rolls · catch the King
Play Open Draft

21 picks: two at each of the 9 positions (C, 1B, 2B, 3B, SS, LF, CF, RF, DH) plus 3 wildcards — matching the 21 seasons Bonds played to set the record. Each batter shows their full totals for that era — seasons played (SZN), average, runs, RBI, hits and steals — with home runs hidden until you draft. You're scored on each player's single best HR season of the era. Eras run from 1920, the Babe Ruth live-ball era, to today.
